stories.headline: Netflix’s ‘Little House On The Prairie’ Rotten Tomatoes Review Score Is In - Forbes
stories.summary: Netflix is going back 50 years for its newest project, a reboot of Little House on the Prairie, last seen on TV in 1974. Here's its Rotten Tomatoes score.
stories.why: A nostalgia play on a show your dad watched signals Netflix betting that millennial men are ready to pay for their childhoods—and it's bombing, which means that market assumption is wrong.

stories.headline: Nick Carter’s Sexual Assault Accuser Melissa Fears Harassment, Threats From His Fans - TMZ
stories.summary: Nick Carter’s sexual assault accuser, Melissa Schuman, fears harassment from the pop star’s fans, TMZ has learned. For his part, the pop star claims Melissa's alleged false claims have caused severe…
stories.why: The Backstreet Boys-adjacent figure facing sexual assault allegations during the #MeToo reckoning shows how accountability finally caught up with '90s pop culture, even for B-tier celebrities most forgot existed.

stories.headline: One runner gored in the face at Spain’s San Fermin bull run festival - AP News
stories.summary: One runner has been gored in the face and many more were fortunate to not be seriously injured during a chaotic bull run at Spain’s San Fermin festival on Saturday. The six bulls and accompanying ste…
stories.why: A man getting gored in the face at a bucket-list festival is the collision between Instagram adventure culture and real danger—it's the moment the algorithm's romanticization meets actual consequences.

stories.headline: Jay-Z’s Yankee Stadium Night One Setlist: Every Song Played at the First Show - Billboard
stories.summary: The star-studded affair included appearances by Beyoncé, Blue Ivy Carter, Nas, Alicia Keys and more.
stories.why: Jay-Z selling out Yankee Stadium with his wife as co-headliner signals hip-hop is now the dominant culture format, not rap's upstart scrapping for respect—this is establishment-level power.

stories.headline: Horoscope for Saturday, July 11, 2026 - Chicago Sun-Times
stories.why: This entry appears to have no summary, so cannot be meaningfully analyzed.

stories.headline: Barbara Ling, Oscar-Winning Production Designer on ‘Once Upon a Time in Hollywood,’ Dies at 73 - The Hollywood Reporter
stories.summary: Her credits also included ‘The Pee-Wee Herman Show,’ ‘Falling Down, ‘The Doors,’ ‘Fried Green Tomatoes’ and ‘Michael.’
stories.why: A production designer who shaped how the '70s and '80s look in modern film just died, meaning the visual language of nostalgia filmmaking lost one of its architects.

stories.headline: Taylor Swift flashes wedding ring 1 week after marrying Travis Kelce - Page Six
stories.summary: Swift playfully flashed her new bling, enjoyed champagne and danced beneath the California sun alongside her new husband.
stories.why: The biggest pop star marrying an NFL star and publicly displaying it is the final merger of entertainment and sports culture—no more separation between Taylor's world and football's world.

stories.headline: Gunther attacks Nick Aldis following WWE Saturday Night’s Main Event announcement - F4W/WON
stories.summary: The issues between Gunther and Nick Aldis are far from over.
stories.why: WWE's narrative tension between its authority figure and its monster heel is heating up, signaling the company's betting that strong storylines (not just wrestlers) drive engagement and ticket sales.
